title: Agua Dulce
description: Named by Pedro Fages in 1772 for its sweet water springs

title: Acton
description: Unincorporated community in the Antelope Valley foothills

title: Bouquet Canyon
description: Canyon and community northeast of Saugus

title: Camulos
description: Historic del Valle rancho area near Piru along SR-126

title: Canyon Country
description: Northeastern Santa Clarita community

title: Castaic
description: Community at the confluence of Castaic Creek and Santa Clara River

title: Green Valley
description: Small community in the hills above Castaic

title: Hasley Canyon
description: Canyon community west of Castaic

title: Lebec
description: Community at the base of Tejon Pass

title: Mint Canyon
description: Canyon corridor connecting SCV to Antelope Valley

title: Newhall
description: Original downtown — named after Henry Mayo Newhall

title: Piru
description: Small community along SR-126 near Rancho Camulos

title: Placerita Canyon
description: Site of California's first gold discovery (1842)

title: Sand Canyon
description: Rural canyon community north of Canyon Country

title: San Francisquito Canyon
description: Canyon where St. Francis Dam was located — disaster site 1928

title: Saugus
description: Named after Saugus, Massachusetts — birthplace of Henry Mayo Newhall

title: Soledad Canyon
description: Canyon corridor; Lang Station (first SP transcontinental junction) located here

title: Stevenson Ranch
description: Planned community west of Valencia

title: Tejon
description: Area near Tejon Pass and Rancho El Tejon

title: Tourney Road Corridor
description: Commercial and office corridor near Magic Mountain Parkway

title: Valencia
description: Master-planned community developed by Newhall Land beginning 1965
